title: approvals — notification classes + categories
description: Eight notification classes serve three audiences: approvers (requested + reminder + cancelled), requesters (granted + rejected + expired), and admins (execution-failed + template-changed). Every notification is templated per (tenant, action_key) via the localization::NotificationTemplate mechanism so tenant admins can tune copy without redeploying. Consent tiers follow the compliance module's four-tier model: `transactional_required` (mandatory; no opt-out) for lifecycle-critical notifications, `functional_optional` (opt-out allowed) for reminders + template-change awareness.

categories.description: You have been asked to approve or reject a request. Includes the action label, requester, subject summary, and a link to the review page.
categories.notes: Cannot be opted out — an approver who has been assigned a pending decision MUST receive the notification. SOC 2 CC6.3 evidence.

categories.description: Nudge for a pending request approaching its expires_at deadline. Cadence controlled by template.reminder_config.intervals_hours (default: 24h, 48h, 72h).
categories.notes: Reminders are transactional_required for the same reason as the initial request notification. Approvers who ignore reminders and let requests expire are what template.on_timeout is FOR.

categories.description: Your request was approved. Includes the final decider, decision comments (if any), and the outcome of the executed action.
Your request was rejected. Includes the rejecting approver's identity + comment (SOC 2 requires the reason).
Your request expired before approvers decided. Consult the tenant's approval policy for next steps. Final state depends on template.on_timeout — the notification names the outcome.
categories.notes: Dual-audience: both the requester AND any approvers who hadn't yet decided receive this so no one wastes time reviewing an expired request.

categories.description: A pending request was withdrawn by its requester (or, rarely, cancelled by a tenant admin on the requester's behalf). Sent to any pending approvers.
P0 — an approved request's underlying action threw during execution. The instance is in a wedged state; platform-ops must intervene via `approvals:remediate-failed-executions`.
categories.notes: Slack channel is #approvals-alerts (platform-owned; not tenant-configurable). Mail goes to the standard ops distribution list.

categories.description: A tenant admin edited an approval template's semantic-behaviour fields (when_expression, groups, on_timeout). Governance-change awareness for other admins in the tenant. Opt-in by default — admins can disable for high-churn tenants.
categories.notes: Fires on ApprovalTemplateVersionBumped, NOT on ApprovalTemplateUpdated (label-only edits don't warrant a notification).

template_hooks.note: Every approval notification is templated per-action-key so tenants can customise per-flow. The localization::NotificationTemplate resolver looks up (tenant_id, category_slug, action_key, locale) → falls back to (tenant_id, category_slug, NULL, locale) → falls back to (NULL, category_slug, NULL, locale). Only the last (platform-shipped default) is guaranteed to exist.
context_variables: approval_instance (id, status, requested_at, expires_at, decided_at, executed_at)
approvers (user summary array — the resolved approvers on pending requirements)
review_url (deep-link to the pending-my-review page for approvers, my-requests for requesters)
